_comment: HBP 1.0 codes S400028 and S400058 map to HBP 2.0 code SO003A, which is the primary procedure code for these guidelines.
procedure_name: Laparoscopic tubal surgeries (for any indication including ectopic pregnancy)
clinical_indications: Management of ectopic pregnancy (with confirmed tubal pregnancy measuring > 35 mm, concurrent intrauterine pregnancy ruled out, significant pain abdomen, and failure of medical management)
Fimbrioplasty for correction of prefimbrial phimosis

clinical_thresholds.note: Confirmed tubal pregnancy for ectopic pregnancy management

preauth.label: Detailed Clinical notes with history, symptoms, signs, examination findings, indication for procedure, planned line of treatment, and advice for admission
Serum Beta human chorionic gonadotropin (hCG) titers

claim.label: Serum Beta hCG level (in case of ectopic pregnancy)
Blood transfusion notes (if blood transfusion was given)

ppd_preauth.q: Are all vitals, detailed history, symptoms, signs, physical examination including local examination, indication for procedure, planned line of treatment and advise for admission documented in Detailed Clinical notes?
Did the clinical presentation, evaluation findings, and imaging/investigations confirm the diagnosis?
Was the clinical presentation, severity, imaging, and laboratory investigations, failure of medical management indicative of surgery (for Ectopic Pregnancy)?
Was clinical presentation and imaging indicative of tubal surgery other than Ectopic pregnancy?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details available?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Was clinical evaluation findings, imaging and/or investigations indicative of surgery?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: Tubal pregnancy is not confirmed to be measuring more than 35 mm, or concurrent intrauterine pregnancy is not ruled out for ectopic pregnancy cases.
Failure of medical management for ectopic pregnancy is not documented or justified.
Clinical presentation, severity, imaging, or laboratory investigations did not indicate the necessity for surgery for ectopic pregnancy.
Clinical presentation or imaging did not indicate the necessity for tubal surgery for non-ectopic indications.
Mandatory Serum Beta hCG titers or USG abdomen/pelvis are missing at pre-authorization.
Histopathological Examination or Detailed Procedure/Operative notes are missing at claim submission.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Facilities with well-equipped operation theatre, anesthesia and anesthetist availability. Laparoscopic facility for laparoscopic procedures.
clinical_key_pointers: Pain abdomen: esp. one sided, dull/sharp/crampy, continuous/intermittent
Past history of: Pelvic Inflammatory Disease, Ectopic pregnancy, Tubal surgery
